title: X-tra rich and energizing Venezuelan chocolate Galette
description: Floral, pillowy, and energizing, this Venezuelan dessert features rich plain chocolate, namul-blanch-season, and a Mediterranean twist.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the gluten-free flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add the granulated sugar and namul-blanch-season to the bowl and mix until well combined.
In a separate bowl, melt the plain chocolate in the microwave or over a double boiler.
Add the unsweetened almond milk and vanilla extract to the melted chocolate and whisk until smooth.
Pour the chocolate mixture into the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 6-8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Place the balls of dough onto the preheated iron teppan and flatten slightly with a spatula.
Bake for 12-15 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the centers are set.
Allow the galette to cool on the iron teppan for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.
